optimization_backlog.title: SQLite mmap_size PRAGMA: memory-mapped reads (128 MiB)
optimization_backlog.notes: One-liner PRAGMA addition. Enables mmap reads for up to 128MiB. Safe in WAL mode. No measurable diff in in-memory benchmarks (expected). File-based reads benefit on repeated page access.

optimization_backlog.title: Cache recent session history in memory
optimization_backlog.notes: load_history() hits SQLite on every Goose agent invocation. An LRU cache per session would avoid repeated reads. Requires more significant code change and thread-safety considerations.

perf_notes: Full workspace build downloads goose from GitHub (network blocked). Use -p opengoose-persistence for local work.
SQLite synchronous=NORMAL in WAL mode is safe and ~12x faster for per-commit writes. PR #39 merged.
libsqlite3-sys with bundled feature - SQLite version is bundled, no system SQLite dependency.
Criterion benchmarks baselines (in-memory WAL, current state 2026-03-11): append_message ~15-16µs, get_active_team ~1µs, load_history(10) ~6µs, load_history(50) ~28µs, set_active_team ~9µs.
In-memory SQLite doesn't show mmap benefit - file-based workloads gain from mmap_size on repeated page reads.
conn.transaction() requires PersistenceError: From<diesel::result::Error> which is already implemented.
All three previous PRs merged quickly by maintainer.
